author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXXXVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of this Psalm is brief and simple, and need not detain us; since we know whose resemblance David wore, and since in him we recognise ourselves also, for we too are members of that Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The whole title is, “To David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see then, what is to David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of the Psalm is wont to tell us what is treated of within it: but in this, since the title informs us not of this, but tells us only to Whom it is chanted, the first verse tells us what is treated of in the whole Psalm, “I will confess to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This confession then let us hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But first I remind you, that the term confession in Scripture, when we speak of confession to God, is used in two senses, of sin, and of praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But confession of sin all know, confession of praise few attend to.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So well known is confession of sin, that, wherever in Scripture we hear the words, “I will confess to Thee, O Lord,” or, “we will confess to Thee,” forthwith, through habitually understanding in this way, our hands hurry to beating our breast: so entirely are men wont not to understand confession to be of aught, save of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But was then our Lord Jesus Christ Himself too a sinner, who saith in the Gospel, “I confess to Thee, Father, Lord of heaven and earth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He goeth on to say what He confesseth, that we might understand His confession to be of praise, not of sin, “I confess to Thee, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because Thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He praised the Father, he praised God, because He despiseth not the humble, but the proud.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And such confession are we now going to hear, of praise of God, of thanksgiving.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With my whole heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My whole heart I lay upon the altar of Thy praise, an whole burnt-offering of praise I offer to Thee.…“I will confess to Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart: for Thou hast heard the words of my mouth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What mouth, save my heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there have we the voice which God heareth, which ear of man knoweth not at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have then a mouth within, there do we ask, thence do we ask, and if we have prepared a lodging or an house for God, there do we speak, there are we heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He is not far from every one of us, for in Him we live, and move, and have our being.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nought maketh thee far off from God, save sin only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cast down the middle wall of sin, and thou art with Him whom thou askest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And before the Angels will I sing unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not before men will I sing, but before the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My song is my joy; but my joy in things below is before men, my joy in things above before the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the wicked knoweth not the joy of the just: “There is no joy, saith my God, to the wicked.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wicked rejoiceth in his tavern, the martyr in his chain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what did that holy Crispina rejoice, whose festival is kept to-day?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She rejoiced when she was being seized, when she was being carried before the judge, when she was being put into prison, when she was being brought forth bound, when she was being lifted up on the scaffold, when she was being heard, when she was being condemned: in all these things she rejoiced; and the wretches thought her wretched, when she was rejoicing before the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will worship toward Thy holy Temple” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What holy Temple?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That where we shall dwell, where we shall worship.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we hasten that we may adore.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our heart is pregnant and cometh to the birth, and seeketh where it may bring forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the place where God is to be worshipped?…“The Temple of God is holy,” saith the Apostle, “which Temple ye are.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But assuredly, as is manifest, God dwelleth in the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when our joy, being in spiritual things, not in earthly, taketh up a song to God, to sing before the Angels, that very assembly of Angels is the Temple of God, we worship toward God’s Temple.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a Church below, there is a Church above also; the Church below, in all the faithful; the Church above, in all the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the God of Angels came down to the Church below, and Angels ministered to Him on earth, while He ministered to us; for, “I came not,” saith He, “to be ministered unto, but to minister.”…The Lord of Angels died for man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “I will worship toward Thy holy Temple;” I mean, not the temple made with hands, but that which Thou hast made for Thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And I will confess to Thy Name in Thy mercy and Thy truth.”…These also which Thou hast given to me, do I according to my power give to Thee in return: mercy, in aiding others; truth, in judging.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By these God aideth us, by these we win God’s favour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly, therefore, “All the ways of the Lord are mercy and truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No other ways are there whereby He can come to us, no other whereby we can come to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou hast magnified Thy holy Name over everything.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of thanksgiving is this, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath magnified His holy Name over Abraham.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of Abraham was born Isaac; over that house God was magnified; then Jacob; God was magnified, who said, “I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then came his twelve sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The name of the Lord was magnified over Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then came the Virgin Mary.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then Christ our Lord, “dying for our sins, rising again for our justification,” filling the faithful with His Holy Spirit, sending forth men to proclaim throughout the Gentiles, “Repent ye,” etc. Behold, “He hath magnified His holy Name above all things.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In what day soever I call upon Thee, do Thou quickly hear me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, “quickly”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Thou hast said, “While yet thou art speaking I will say, Lo, here I am.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, “quickly”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because now I seek not earthly happiness, I have learnt holy longings from the New Testament.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I seek not earth, nor earthly abundance, nor temporal health, nor the overthrow of my enemies, nor riches, nor rank: nought of these do I seek: therefore “quickly hear me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since Thou hast taught me what to seek, grant what I seek.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see then what he seeketh, with what right he hath said, “quickly hear me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what seekest thou, that thou shouldest quickly be heard?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt multiply me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In many ways may multiplication be understood.…For men are multiplied in their soul with cares: a man seemeth to be multiplied in soul, in whom vices even are multiplied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is the multiplication of want, not of fulness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then dost thou desire, thou who hast said, “quickly hear me,” and hast withdrawn thyself entirely from the body, from every earthly thing, from every earthly desire, so as to say to God, “Thou shalt multiply me in my soul”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Explain yet further what thou desirest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou shalt multiply me, saith he, in my soul “with virtue.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all the kings of the earth confess to Thee, O Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So shall it be, and so it is, and that daily; and it is shown that it was not said in vain, save that it was future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But neither let them, when they confess to Thee, when they praise Thee, desire earthly things of Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what shall the kings of the earth desire?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have they not already sovereignty?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever more a man desire on earth, sovereignty is the highest point of his desire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What more can he desire?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It must needs be some loftier eminence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps the loftier it is, the more dangerous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And therefore the more exalted kings are in earthly eminence, the more ought they to humble themselves before God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do they do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because they have heard all the words of Thy mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a certain nation were hidden the Law and the Prophets, “all the words of Thy mouth:” in the Jewish nation alone were “all the words of Thy mouth,” the nation which the Apostle praiseth, saying, “What advantage hath the Jew?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Much every way; chiefly because that unto them were committed the oracles of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These were the words of God.…What meant Gideon’s fleece?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is like the nation of the Jews in the midst of the world, which had the grace of sacraments, not indeed openly manifested, but hidden in a cloud, or in a veil, like the dew in the fleece.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The time came when the dew was to be manifested in the floor; it was manifested, no longer hidden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ alone is the sweetness of dew: Him alone thou recognisest not in Scripture, for whom Scripture was written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet, “they have heard all the words of thy mouth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let them sing in the paths of the Lord, that great is the glory of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let all the kings of the earth sing in the paths of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In what paths?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those that are spoken of above, “in Thy mercy and Thy truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not then the kings of the earth be proud, let them be humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then let them sing in the ways of the Lord, if they be humble: let them love, and they shall sing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know travellers that sing; they sing, and hasten to reach the end of their journey.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are evil songs, such as belong to the old man; to the new man belongeth a new song.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then the kings of the earth too walk in Thy paths, let them walk and sing in Thy paths.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sing what? that “great is the glory of the Lord,” not of kings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how he willed that kings should sing on their way, humbly bearing the Lord, not lifting themselves up against the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if they lift themselves up, what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord is high, and hath respect unto the lowly” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do kings then desire that He have respect unto them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them be humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then? if they lift themselves up to pride, can they escape His eyes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest perchance, because thou hast heard, “He hath respect unto the lowly,” thou choose to be proud, and say in thy soul, God hath respect unto the lowly, He hath not respect unto me, I will do what I will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O foolish one! wouldest thou say this, if thou knewest what thou oughtest to love?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, even if God willeth not to see thee, dost thou not fear this very thing, that He willeth not to see thee?…The lofty then, it seemeth, He hath not respect unto, for it is the lowly He respecteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The lofty”—what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He considereth from afar.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then gaineth the proud?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To be seen from afar, not to escape being seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And think not that thou must needs be safe on that account, for that He seeth less clearly, who seeth thee from afar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou indeed seest not clearly, what thou seest from afar; God, although He see thee from afar, seeth thee perfectly, yet is He not with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This thou gainest, not that thou art less perfectly seen, but that thou art not with Him by whom thou art seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what doth the lowly gain?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a contrite heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the proud then lift himself up as much as he will, certainly God dwelleth on high, God is in heaven: wishest thou that He come nigh to thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humble thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the higher will He be above thee, the more thou liftest thyself up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I walk in the midst of tribulation, Thou shalt revive me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: True it is: whatsoever tribulation thou art in, confess, call on Him; He freeth thee, He reviveth thee.…Love the other life, and thou shalt see that this life is tribulation, whatever prosperity it shine with, whatever delights it abound and overflow with; since not yet have we that joy most safe and free from all temptation, which God reserveth for us in the end, without doubt it is tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us understand then what tribulation he meaneth here too, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as though he said, “If perchance there shall any tribulation have befallen me, Thou shalt free me therefrom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If I walk,” etc.; that is, otherwise Thou wilt not revive me, unless I walk in the midst of tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast stretched forth Thine hand over the wrath of mine enemies, and Thy right hand hath made me safe.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let mine enemies rage: what can they do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They can take my money, strip, proscribe, banish me; afflict me with grief and tortures; at last, if they be allowed, even kill me: can they do aught more?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But over that which mine enemies can do, Thou hast stretched forth Thine hand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mine enemies cannot separate me from Thee: but Thou avengest me the more, the more Thou as yet delayest.…Yet not to make me despair; for it follows, “and Thy right hand hath made me safe.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou, Lord, shalt recompense for me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I recompense not: Thou shalt recompense.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let mine enemies rage their full: Thou shalt recompense what I cannot.…“Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves,” saith the Apostle, “but rather give place unto wrath; for it is written, Vengeance is Mine, I will repay, saith the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is here another sense not to be neglected, perhaps even to be preferred.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord” Christ, “Thou shalt repay for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I, if I repay, have seized; Thou hast paid what Thou hast not seized.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lord, Thou shalt “repay for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold Him repaying for us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They came to Him, who exacted tribute: they used to demand as tribute a didrachma, that is, two drachmas for one man; they came to the Lord to pay tribute; or rather, not to Him, but to His disciples, and they said to them, “Doth not your Master pay tribute?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They came and told Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith unto Peter, “lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up: and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a stater: that take, and give for Me and thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The first that riseth from the sea, is the First-begotten from the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In His mouth we find two didrachmas, that is, four drachmas: in His mouth we find the four Gospels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By those four drachmas we are free from the claims of this world, by the four Evangelists we remain no longer debtors; for there the debt of all our sins is paid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then hath repaid for us, thanks to His mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He owed nothing: He repaid not for Himself: He repaid for us.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, Thy mercy is for everlasting.”…Not for a time only do I desire to be freed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy mercy is for everlasting,” wherewith Thou hast freed the martyrs, and so hast quickly taken them from this life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Despise not Thou the works of Thine own hands.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I say not, Lord, “despise not the works of my hands:” of mine own works I boast not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I sought,” indeed, “the Lord with my hands in the night season before Him, and have not been deceived;” but yet I praise not the works of mine own hands; I fear lest, when Thou shalt look into them, Thou find more sins in them than deserts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold in me Thy Work, not mine: for mine if Thou seest, Thou condemnest; Thine, if Thou seest, Thou crownest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whatever good works there be of mine, from Thee are they to me; and so they are more Thine than mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore whether in regard that we are men, or in regard that we have been changed and justified from our iniquity, Lord, “despise not Thou the works of Thine own hands.”
